text.title: Rust Error Handling Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns: library/app confusion, untyped strings, lost source chains, prod-panic patterns.

antipattern.symptom: A published library returns `anyhow::Result<T>`; downstream tries to match on a specific failure but cannot because `anyhow::Error` is opaque `Box<dyn Error>` under the hood.
antipattern.root-cause: Author wanted boilerplate-free errors and reached for `anyhow`. The cost is paid by every downstream caller who now must downcast.
antipattern.fix: Switch library to `thiserror` enum. Keep `anyhow` only in `examples/` and tests. Mark this in `Cargo.toml` keywords so users see the contract.

antipattern.symptom: Custom error is `pub struct MyError(pub String)`. Lower-level errors get `.to_string()`'d into it; the original error type is lost.
antipattern.root-cause: Author skipped `thiserror` because the type seemed simple. The shortcut throws away source chains and `Display` formatting symmetry.
antipattern.fix: Replace with `#[derive(thiserror::Error)]` enum. Wrap upstream errors with `#[from]` or `#[source]`. Display strings via `#[error("...")]`.

antipattern.symptom: Error variant captures source as `String` (`Variant(String)`) instead of typed (`Variant(#[source] io::Error)`). `tracing` log shows the error but cannot walk causes.
antipattern.root-cause: `#[source]` keeps the original type and lets `.source()` walk the chain. Stringifying flattens the chain to a single line.</rationale>
antipattern.fix: Change variant to `Variant(#[from] io::Error)` (or `#[source]` if you also need data). Update log instrumentation: `tracing::error!(error = ?e)` walks the chain when present.

antipattern.symptom: Production binary panics in production with `thread 'main' panicked at 'called Result::unwrap() on an Err value'`. No context.
antipattern.root-cause: Prototype `.unwrap()` calls left behind. Default clippy config does not deny these.
antipattern.fix: Add `templates/clippy.toml`: `unwrap_used = "deny"`, `expect_used = "deny"`. Run `cargo clippy -- -D clippy::unwrap_used -D clippy::expect_used` in CI. Replace each call with `?` + context.
